steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The expression $(x+8)^2 + 14(x+8) + 49$ is a trinomial with a compound base. Recognize whether it fits the perfect-square pattern $u^2 + 14u + 49 = (u+7)^2$ by setting $u = x+8$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $(x+15)^2$: substituting $u = x+8$ gives $(u+7)^2 = (x+8+7)^2 = (x+15)^2$. ✓
  • Choice B — $(x+8)^2$: ignores the $+14u+49$ terms; only correct if those were zero. ✗
  • Choice C — $(x+9)^2$: would require $u+1$, not $u+7$. ✗
  • Choice D — $(x+57)^2$: no combination of the terms produces a constant of 57. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
